answer: Tippen Sie auf die Schaltfläche „Info“, um mehr zu erfahren.

info:
German pronunciation is systematic and rule-driven, much closer to spelling than English.
Vowel length (long vs short) matters more than stress, and vowels are pure — they do not glide like English ones.
Consonants are fully articulated, especially at the ends of words, where voiced sounds become voiceless.
Every syllable is pronounced clearly, with far less reduction than English.
If you finish consonants cleanly and keep vowels steady, German becomes predictable and easy to parse.
prompt:
Focus on pronuncation
Tap the "info" button now and for each letter for more info
answer:
Focus on pronuncation
Tap the "info" button now and for each letter for more info

info:
A a. 
Like ah in father. Can be short (Mann) or long (Name). English mistake: turning it into æ (“cat”)

B b. 
Like English b, but:. At end of words → p. lieb → leep

C c. 
Rare alone. Usually in ch or ck. English mistake: expecting see or k

D d. 
Like English d. Word-final → t. und → unt

E e. 
Short: like bed. Long: like ay but purer (See). At word endings -e is pronounced (bitte)

F f. 
Same as English.

G g. 
Hard g normally. Final g often sounds like k or soft ch. English mistake: soft j sound
answer: G - Guten - Ge "gay" - Tag GOO-ten tahk

info:
H h. 
Strongly pronounced at syllable start. Silent after vowels (marks vowel length). sehen

I i. 
Short: bit. Long: ee (Bier). Never like English eye

J j. 
Always y (ja). Never English j. One of the biggest traps

K k. 
Hard k, fully released. English mistake: under-articulating.

L l. 
Clear l, never dark. English mistake: using ɫ.

M m / N n. 
Same as English, but cleaner.

M m / N n. 
Same as English, but cleaner.

O o. 
Short: like lot. Long: pure oh. English mistake: adding a glide (ohw)

P p. 
Strong p, often aspirated. English mistake: too soft.

Q q. 
Always qu = kv. Qualität. Never kw

R r. 
Many variants:. uvular (throat). rolled
answer: R - Rose - Er "air" (with German r) - roh-zeh

info:
S s. 
At word start before vowel → z. Sonne → Zonne. Elsewhere → s

T t. 
Clean t. More dental than English. English mistake: flapping or softening

U u. 
Short: like put. Long: oo (gut). English mistake: turning it into uh

V v. 
Pronounced f. English mistake: v.

W w. 
Pronounced v. English mistake: w.

X x. 
Pronounced ks. English mistake: gz. Y y

Y y. 
Usually like ü. English mistake: why.

Z z. 
Always ts. Never z. English mistake: voicing it

Ä ä. 
Like eh / air. Often clearer than English a. English mistake: ignoring it as “just an a”
answer: Ä - Äpfel - Ä (Umlaut-A) "eh" with mouth wider - EP-fel

info:
Ö ö. 
No English equivalent. Lips rounded, tongue like eh. English mistake: saying er or oh
answer: Ö - Öl - Ö (Umlaut-O) Like "ur" in "burn" (rounded) - oel

info:
Ü ü. 
No English equivalent. Lips rounded, tongue like ee. English mistake: saying oo or ee
answer: Ü - Über - Ü (Umlaut-U) Like French "u" or German "ü" - EW-ber
goal_words: Ü (Umlaut-U) Like French "u" or German "ü" - EW-ber

info:
ß. 
Always s. Indicates long vowel before it.
answer: ß - Straße - Eszett or scharfes S "ss" sound - SHTRAH-seh
goal_words: Eszett or scharfes S "ss" sound - SHTRAH-seh

info:
SCH. 
Like sh. Very stable.
Schule → SHOO-luh (school)

SP / ST. 
At word start:. shp / sht. sprechen, Straße

CH. 
Two sounds:. After a, o, u → harsh throat kh. After e, i, ä, ö, ü → soft hiss
Bach → bahkh
ich → ikh (soft)

EI. 
Pronounced eye. English mistake: ee. IE
mein → mine

IE. 
Pronounced ee. English mistake: eye. 
Bier → beer
prompt:
IE. 
Pronounced ee. English mistake: eye. 
Bier → beer Bier
answer:
IE. 
Pronounced ee. English mistake: eye. 
Bier → beer Bier

info:
Vowel length.  This is the #1 pronunciation mistake.
German cares more about long vs short vowels than about stress.
bieten (long ee) ≠ bitten (short i)
Staat ≠ Stadt
Ofen ≠ offen
prompt: Vowel length. bieten. bitten. Staat. Stadt. Ofen. offen
answer: Vowel length. bieten. bitten. Staat. Stadt. Ofen. offen
audio_dir: bieten. bitten. Staat. Stadt. Ofen. offen

info:
Devoice final consonants. English keeps voicing; German doesn’t.
Tag → tak
und → unt
lieb → leep
prompt: Devoice final consonants. Tag. und. lieb
answer: Devoice final consonants. Tag. und. lieb

info:
German R varies widely: throat, rolled, tapped. pick one non-English R and be consistent.
A light uvular R is safest.
rot (red)
Brot (bread)
reisen (to travel)
prompt: German R varies widely. rot. Brot. reisen.
answer: German R varies widely. rot. Brot. reisen.

info:
English speakers often drop weak endings.
bitte → BIT-tuh
machen → MAH-khen
prompt: English speakers often drop weak endings. bitte. machen.
answer: English speakers often drop weak endings. bitte. machen.

info:
English slides vowels. German parks on them. Don't turn German vowels into English diphthongs:
Name, so, boot
prompt: Don't turn German vowels into English diphthongs: name, so, boot
answer: Don't turn German vowels into English diphthongs: name, so, boot

info:
German gives every syllable a job. English fires half of them.
Wir haben keine Zeit
prompt: German does every syllable. Wir haben keine Zeit
answer: German does every syllable. Wir haben keine Zeit
